comment_text.sn5.4:1.2: A different set of verses are attributed to her at Thig 6.8:0.3.
comment_text.sn5.4:2.1: This challenge and response is similar to the verses of Khemā (Thig 6.3).
comment_text.sn5.4:2.4: Explained by the commentary as one-skinned and two-skinned drums, a stringed instrument, a wind instrument, and metal percussion such as gongs, cymbals, etc.
comment_text.sn5.4:6.1: In this verse she declares that, not only has she transcended attachment to the senses, she has even surmounted attachment to meditative absorption.

root_text.sn5.4:1.2: Atha kho vijayā bhikkhunī pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā …pe…
root_text.sn5.4:1.4: Atha kho māro pāpimā vijayāya bhikkhuniyā bhayaṁ chambhitattaṁ lomahaṁsaṁ uppādetukāmo samādhimhā cāvetukāmo yena vijayā bhikkhunī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā vijayaṁ bhikkhuniṁ gāthāya ajjhabhāsi:
root_text.sn5.4:3.2: “ko nu khvāyaṁ manusso vā amanusso vā gāthaṁ bhāsatī”ti?
root_text.sn5.4:3.4: “māro kho ayaṁ pāpimā mama bhayaṁ chambhitattaṁ lomahaṁsaṁ uppādetukāmo samādhimhā cāvetukāmo gāthaṁ bhāsatī”ti.
root_text.sn5.4:3.5: Atha kho vijayā bhikkhunī “māro ayaṁ pāpimā” iti viditvā māraṁ pāpimantaṁ gāthāhi paccabhāsi:
root_text.sn5.4:7.1: Atha kho māro pāpimā “jānāti maṁ vijayā bhikkhunī”ti dukkhī dummano tatthevantaradhāyīti.

translation_text.sn5.4:1.2: Then the nun Vijayā robed up in the morning …
translation_text.sn5.4:1.3: and sat at the root of a tree for the day’s meditation.
translation_text.sn5.4:1.4: Then Māra the Wicked, wanting to make the nun Vijayā feel fear, terror, and goosebumps, wanting to make her fall away from immersion, went up to her and addressed her in verse:
translation_text.sn5.4:2.2: and I’m a youth in my prime.
translation_text.sn5.4:2.3: Come, my lady, let us enjoy
translation_text.sn5.4:2.4: the music of a five-piece band.”
translation_text.sn5.4:3.2: “Who’s speaking this verse, a human or a non-human?”
translation_text.sn5.4:3.4: “This is Māra the Wicked, wanting to make me feel fear, terror, and goosebumps, wanting to make me fall away from immersion!”
translation_text.sn5.4:3.5: Then Vijayā, knowing that this was Māra the Wicked, replied to him in verse:
translation_text.sn5.4:4.3: I hand them right back to you, Māra,
translation_text.sn5.4:4.4: for I have no use for them.
translation_text.sn5.4:5.3: I’m horrified and repelled by it,
translation_text.sn5.4:6.1: There are beings in the realm of luminous form,
translation_text.sn5.4:6.2: and others stuck in the formless.
translation_text.sn5.4:7.1: Then Māra the Wicked, thinking, “The nun Vijayā knows me!” miserable and sad, vanished right there.
